quotes.text: It's quite valuable to have the courage and the confidence to say, 'No, fuck off, leave me alone, thank you very much.'
quotes.speaker: Helen Mirren
quotes.context: In a 2013 Daily Mail interview, the Oscar-winning actress said that if she'd had a daughter, "fuck off" is the first phrase she would have taught her, to give her the confidence to set boundaries.

quotes.text: People meet me and go 'oh, the wholesome dad!' and I have to explain that the guy on television and the guy at the two a.m. show are legally separate fucking individuals. One of them coaches little league. The other one got thrown out of a Denny's for a bit about the dessert menu that I stand behind to this day. You cannot put that man anywhere near a brunch.
quotes.speaker: Bob Saget
quotes.context: On his stand-up vs. TV reputation, a 2014 podcast appearance
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the 'wholesome dad on TV, animal on stage' contrast was Saget's entire deal. The tell: the real Bob would've told you EXACTLY what the dessert-menu bit was, in detail nobody asked for.

quotes.text: A couple nights ago, I was licking jelly off my boyfriend's penis. And I thought, "Oh my God — I'm turning into my mother!"
quotes.speaker: Sarah Silverman
quotes.context: From her 2005 stand-up special "Sarah Silverman: Jesus Is Magic." Her signature sweet-faced delivery lands a graphic sex image as the setup, with the punchline being the realization she's inheriting her mother's habits.

quotes.text: I went to one of these fancy joints where the portions are the size of a postage stamp and the bill's the size of a phone book. The waiter goes 'and how did we find everything?' I said WE didn't find a goddamn thing — I'm still looking, bring a flashlight. Put a real plate in front of me before I eat the centerpiece, the candle, and the little prick who priced the calamari.
quotes.speaker: Buddy Hackett
quotes.context: A raunchy 1970s Las Vegas nightclub set
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the borscht-belt restaurant tirade escalating into eating the furniture is Buddy Hackett's nightclub register. The tell: the real Buddy would've detoured into something genuinely obscene halfway through and never found his way back to the calamari.

quotes.text: I tried one of those standing desks. You know what a standing desk is for a man my size? It's called STANDING, and it's a goddamn cardio event. We had the technology the whole time, we just kept sitting on it. I stood up for one meeting and three coworkers asked if I was okay. I said no, Brenda, I'm not okay, I'm WORKING OUT, and if I go down you'd better catch me or update your résumé.
quotes.speaker: Ralphie May
quotes.context: On modern office culture, a 2015 stand-up taping
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the self-deprecating size humor with a sudden named target is the Ralphie May move. The tell: the real Ralphie would've gone darker and more personal, and Brenda would not have walked out of it upright.

quotes.text: My beauty routine? I get up, I put on my face, and I apologize to it. I told my plastic surgeon to do whatever the hell he wanted — he took one look and billed me for a full demolition and a permit. My husband Fang said I looked refreshed. I said Fang, you've been asleep since Tuesday and you wouldn't notice if I came to bed as a different damn woman.
quotes.speaker: Phyllis Diller
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the self-roasting beauty jokes and the 'Fang' husband bit are pure Phyllis Diller. The tell: the real Phyllis fired these as rapid one-liners; this one lets the metaphor sprawl past her clip.
